extraction.one_line_summary: BEVIN Paddy won with a late-stage acceleration from 400m after Taaramäe's solo break was caught, defending race leader Dennis took third for bonus seconds.
tactical_patterns: Verona attacked on flat sections of final climb rather than steep gradient, gained 9 seconds with 2km from summit.
Taaramäe launched solo move on descent after regrouping, nearly held off reduced bunch sprint with less than 500m remaining.
Israel Premier Tech placed Hamiltons in break to vacuum KOM points uncontested while saving Bevin for finale.
Jumbo-Visma controlled entire stage for Dennis despite weak GC lead, prioritizing jersey defense over stage ambitions.

rider_signals.BEVIN Paddy: positive: demonstrated explosive final 400m acceleration capability despite being from breakaway-supporting team, now sits second on GC
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: negative: well-positioned entering final 400m but couldn't respond to Bevin's acceleration despite fresh legs
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: finished third in reduced sprint despite being marked as race leader, actively hunting bonus seconds rather than defending
rider_signals.MÄDER Gino: neutral: initiated counterattacks on Sidera climb but lacked acceleration to sustain move, suggesting form building not peaking
rider_signals.VERONA Carlos: neutral: attacked on flat rather than steep terrain showing tactical creativity but insufficient power advantage
